segments.s389-g41: ببخشید here is a past tense narrative verb (3rd person plural) meaning 'they bestowed/gave' rather than 'forgive' or an imperative; Persian often places the verb before its objects in elevated/poetic style. گنجی is an indefinite direct object ('a treasure'), marked by the -ی suffix; بر اسفندیار is a prepositional phrase indicating the recipient, with بر used in the sense 'upon/to' after giving verbs.
segments.s389-g42: یکی is an indefinite numeral 'one / a certain' placed before the noun; تاج is the head noun 'crown'. پر + گوهر is an adjectival phrase where پر means 'full (of)' and governs the following noun 'گوهر' ('gems'); شاهوار is a postpositive adjective 'regal/lofty' modifying تاج. The whole phrase apposes/specifies the gift just mentioned.
segments.s389-g43: خروشی is the noun 'a roar/clamor' functioning as the subject. برآمد is a past intransitive verb 'arose/issued forth', and ز درگاه شاه is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from the king's threshold/court' showing source; the order Noun–Verb–PP is typical in classical Persian narration.
segments.s389-g44: شد is the past tense of شدن 'became/was' used here to state that something came into being. از گرد is a compound prepositional idea 'from/about/around', خورشید تابان is a noun plus adjective 'the shining sun', and سیاه is an adjective ('black/dark') used predicatively; poetically an adjective like سیاه can stand for 'darkness' or describe the change enacted by شد. The phrase as a whole describes a darkening/ominous appearance around the shining sun.
segments.s389-g45: This clause shows classical Persian word order where source and destination prepositional phrases precede or surround the verb: ز ایوان ('from the iwan/porch') and به دشت ('to the plain') frame آمد ('came'), while اسفندیار (the subject) appears after the verb. ز is the poetic form of 'from', ایوان is the place 'iwan/porch', and به marks the destination.
segments.s389-g46: سپاهی is a noun meaning 'soldiers/army' and functions as the object of گزید, the past-tense verb 'chose/selected'. از در کارزار is a prepositional phrase indicating origin or locus ('from the gate/entrance of the battlefield' or 'from among those at the battle-gate'); in poetic narrative such phrases can mark where the troops are drawn or mustered.
segments.s389-g1: ازان پس is a temporal adverbial ('thereupon/after that') placed before the verb; بیامد is a simple past (3rd person singular) 'came'; به + noun (به پرده‌سرای) marks motion toward/into the place ('into the court‑chamber'). Word order in classical Persian often places the temporal phrase before the verb and the directional phrase after it.
segments.s389-g2: ز هرگونه is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in/from every kind / in every way'; انداخت here is a transitive past 'cast / put (forth, i.e. proposed or offered)' with رای as its object; با شاه رای literally 'with the king, counsel' meaning 'proposed counsel to the king.' The construction places the manner phrase (ز هرگونه) before the verb انداخت.
segments.s389-g3: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; لهراسپ is a proper name. وز here represents a concatenation of و + ز ('and from'), i.e. 'and from'; کین is 'grudge/vengeance' and فرشیدورد is a personal name. The phrase strings two 'from' phrases together: 'from Lohrasp and from (the) grudge of Farshidvard.'
segments.s389-g4: ازان here means 'of those / from those' and introduces the noun phrase نامداران روز نبرد 'the renowned ones of the day of battle' (نامداران = 'renowned/illustrious', روز = 'day', نبرد = 'battle'). The whole phrase describes who or whence those celebrated warriors are.
segments.s389-g5: بدو گفت is literally 'to him he said' (بدو = 'to him/unto it', گفت = past 'said'); گشتاسپ is the speaker's name. کای زورمند is a vocative: کای = 'O' (an archaic/vocative particle) + زورمند = 'mighty one'. Classical Persian often places the indirect object or directional pronoun (بدو) before گفت.
segments.s389-g6: تو is the 2nd‑person singular subject 'you'; شادانی here functions as a predicative (you are glad/joyful)—in older Persian a noun or adjectival form can serve as the predicate for 'to be'; و links the clauses; خواهرانت is 'your sisters' (possessive suffix -ت); به بند means 'in bonds' or 'in fetters' with به indicating state/place and بند = 'fetter/bond.'
segments.s389-g7: خنک is a lofty/poetic evaluative word here ('blessed/fortunate') rather than a literal 'cool' (the sense in the lexicon list does not capture the poetical use); آنک is a relative 'who/that'; بر + گه ('بر کینه گه') = 'at/on the occasion/place of vengeance' (بر = on/upon; گه = place/occasion); کشته شد is passive past 'was killed' (کشته = 'killed', شد used as auxiliary to form the passive).
segments.s389-g8: نه is the negation 'not'; در چنگ = 'in the grasp/clutches' (در = 'in', چنگ = 'claw/grip' used metaphorically); ترکان = 'the Turks' (agent/possessors of that grasp); سرگشته شد = 'became bewildered / was turned about' with شد again functioning as auxiliary/passive marker. The clause negates that state as being praiseworthy compared to dying in vengeance.
segments.s389-g17: سپارم (first word) is a 1st-person singular present/subjunctive form meaning 'I entrust / I give over'; Persian often places the verb before the object in poetry. ترا is the clitic object 'you' (here written as ت + را) with را the definite/object marker attached to the clitic; تاج شاهنشهی is the object 'the imperial crown' (noun + adjectival/epithet). The whole segment is a short main clause meaning 'I entrust you the imperial crown.'
segments.s389-g18: همان is a demonstrative ('that/the same') pointing back to the crown. گنج here is concrete 'treasure.' بی‌رنج is a prefixed adjective 'without toil / untroubled' modifying گنج. و is the conjunction 'and', linking the two appositive phrases; تخت مهی is a compound epithet 'moon‑like throne' (تخت = throne, مهی a poetic epithet meaning 'moonlike' or 'beautiful'). The segment apposes two splendid possessions to the crown.
segments.s389-g19: مرا is the topical/experiencer expression 'for me' (here shown as م + را in the segment tokens), fronted for emphasis. جایگاه پرستش is the noun phrase 'place/status of worship'. بس است is the predicate 'is enough' (بس = enough; است = copula). The clause literally reads 'For me, a place of worship is sufficient.'
segments.s389-g20: نه is the particle of negation 'not'. فرزند من is a possessive construction where the pronoun follows the noun ('my child'). نزد دیگر کس است is a stative locative/predicative phrase meaning 'is (in the presence of) any other person' — here negated by نه, so 'is not with any other person' or 'I have no other child with anyone.'
segments.s389-g21: چنین is an adverb/demonstrative 'thus/so'. پاسخ is the noun 'answer/reply' and آورد is the past tense verb 'brought/uttered'. Proper name اسفندیار is the subject and appears after the verb in the poetic SOV order. The clause reports that Esfandiyar gave such a reply.
segments.s389-g22: که introduces the content clause ('that'). بی‌تو is the prepositional/adverbial phrase 'without you'. مبیناد is an archaic/poetic present/subjunctive form meaning 'appears / is seen' (here used impersonally to indicate existence/appearance). کس روزگار is the noun phrase 'any person in the world/time' (روزگار = world/time/fate); together the clause means 'that without you no one appears in the world.'
segments.s389-g23: به پیش (prepositional compound) means 'before/in the presence of'. پدر من is the possessive 'my father' (pronoun follows noun). یکی is an emphatic numeral/adverb 'one / merely' and بنده‌ام is the predication 'I am a servant' (بنده plus the 1st‑person suffix ‑ام). The whole phrase modestly asserts 'I am only a servant before my father.'
segments.s389-g24: روان را places 'the soul' as the patient/topic with the object marker را. به فرمانش is an agentive/instrumental phrase 'by/with his command' (فرمان + possessive suffix ش = 'his command'). آگنده‌ام is a stative verb/adjective + copula 'I am filled' (آگنده = filled, with the suffix ‑ام 'I am'). Together: 'My soul is filled by his command' or 'my soul is filled with obedience to his command.'
segments.s389-g9: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بینند is a 3rd-person present/subjunctive form 'they see' used here with a general/future sense. ما را is the object with the enclitic object marker را ('us'), and تخت بر + نشست expresses 'seated on the throne' (بر تخت = 'upon the throne'; نشست = 'sitting / sat'). The clause literally reads 'when they see us sitting on the throne'.
segments.s389-g10: چه introduces a rhetorical question 'what (will)'. گوید is the present/subjunctive of 'to say' used to ask what someone will utter; classical Persian often uses present forms for future/hypothetical. کسی 'someone' is modified by the archaic relative/relative-like particle کو here meaning 'who/that' (classical usage), بود is the past copula 'was', and زیر دست is an idiom 'under the hand' meaning 'in someone's power / subordinate'. The whole: 'what will someone who was under (your) hand say?'
segments.s389-g11: بگریم is the 1st-person present/subjunctive/optative form 'I shall/ will weep' (classical Persian uses it for intention or determination). برین is composed of بر + ین (an older/poetic form of این 'this') yielding 'over this' or 'about this', and ننگ 'shame'. تا + زنده‌ام 'while/as long as I am alive' gives the temporal limit. Together: 'I will weep over this shame as long as I live.'
segments.s389-g12: به here marks direction/impact ('into/to'). مغز اندرون is a poetic compound 'the inner core / innermost mind' (مغز = core/brain, اندرون = within). آتش افگنده‌ام is perfective: افگنده (past participle 'cast/struck') + ـام = 'I have cast / I have set on fire'. The image is metaphorical: 'I have cast fire into my inmost core.'
segments.s389-g13: پذیرفتم is the past tense 'I accepted / I consented'. از + کردگار بلند is a prepositional phrase 'from the High/Lofty Creator' (کردگار = Creator/God, بلند = lofty). The verb comes first (typical in poetry), followed by the source marked by از.
segments.s389-g14: که‌گر is a conditional 'if' (archaic form). تو is the 2nd‑person pronoun, به توران شوی uses شوی (present/subjunctive 'you become / you go') with به marking motion/arrival 'to Turan', and بی‌گزند is a predicate adjective 'unharmed / safe'. The clause reads 'if you go to Turan unharmed' or 'if you become (be) in Turan unharmed.'
segments.s389-g15: به مردی شوی = 'you become a man' (به + noun + شوی, where شوی is 'you become'). در دم اژدها is a temporal/locative phrase 'in the very moment/breath of the dragon' (در = in, دم = moment/breath, اژدها = dragon), indicating the crisis/time of peril when manhood is proved.
segments.s389-g16: کنی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive 'you make / you do' (here future/optative sense 'you will set free'). خواهران را — the object 'your sisters' marked by را — and ز ترکان رها is a prepositional phrase 'free from the Turks' (ز = from, رها = free/release). Word order places the object before the verb as is common in Persian poetry.
segments.s389-g33: برفتنت is a nominalized/compound form: بر (on/upon) + رفتن (to go) with the pronominal suffix -ت, literally 'your going/departure'. یزدان پناه تو is a noun phrase 'God [be] refuge for you' (پناه = refuge/protection, تو = you, vocative/emphatic). باد is the optative particle 'may' placed after the wished-for phrase; word order (noun phrase + باد) is a standard poetic optative construction.
segments.s389-g34: به introduces the temporal/occasional phrase 'on/upon the ...'; باز آمدن is the verbal noun 'coming again/return', and تخت گاه تو is the noun phrase 'your throne-seat' (تخت = throne, گاه here part of the compound meaning seat/place/time). باد again is the optative 'may'. The whole segment is another optative clause: 'May (it be) on the return of your throne.'
segments.s389-g35: بخواند is a third-person singular verb (here future/intentive sense) from خواندن used in the sense 'to call/summon' (classical word order allows the verb before the time phrase). آن زمان is the temporal adverbial 'at that time/then'. لشگر is the direct object 'army', and از هر سوی is a prepositional phrase 'from every direction' specifying origin.
segments.s389-g36: به جایی = 'to a place' (به + جایی, destination). که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'. بد modifies موبدی‌گر ('bad/evil mobad/priest'); موبدی‌گر is the agent 'mobad' (priest/magus) possibly with an agentive/derivative ending. گوی here is an archaic/present form meaning 'says/indicates' ('گوی' functioning as a verb 'he says'), so the clause means 'to the place that the evil mobad says/points out.'
segments.s389-g37: ازیشان is از + ایشان 'from them'. گزیده is the past participle 'selected' (here governing the following numeral phrase). The phrase ده و دو هزار is a classical numeral construction meaning 'twelve thousand' (the numeral cluster follows گزیده). Word order places the source 'از ایشان' before the participle.
segments.s389-g38: سواران is the head noun 'horsemen/riders'. مرد افگن و کینه‌دار are post-nominal modifiers: مرد (man/person, here 'men' or 'valiant men') plus the compound/epithet افگن (a formative meaning 'causing/producing' so here 'death-dealing'/'deadly') and the adjective کینه‌دار 'full of hatred/resentful'. Adjectives follow the noun in this classical order.
segments.s389-g39: بر ایشان is a dative phrase 'upon them/to them' (بر used as a preposition). ببخشید is the past tense 'he gave/bestowed' (بخشید here = to give, not 'forgive'). گنج درم are the direct objects 'treasure and dirhams' (two kinds of wealth given). The verb precedes its objects in this classical poetic order.
segments.s389-g40: نکرد is the negated past 'he did not (do/act)'. ایچ کس را ('to/for no one') uses ایچ = 'no/none' and کس = 'person', with را marking the (affected) object. به بخشش دژم is a prepositional phrase 'in (the) giving, sullen' where بخشش = 'giving/grant' and دژم = 'sullen/grim'; together they mean 'sullen in giving'. The clause thus states he was not sullen/stingy toward any person in his giving.
segments.s389-g25: فدای تو دارم is literally 'a sacrifice for you I have'—فدای is a noun/adjectival phrase meaning 'sacrifice for (you)' placed before the verb; دارم is the first‑person present 'I have' (subject omitted). تن و جان خویش is the nominal complement specifying what is offered: تن 'body' and جان 'life/soul', with خوی + ش functioning as the possessive/reflexive element 'own/his (here: my own)'.
segments.s389-g26: نخواهم is the negative first‑person form of خواستن and here means 'I do not want / I shall not desire' (used like a present/future negation). The nouns سر و تخت و فرمان خویش are a coordinated list ('head / throne / command/authority, my own'); راک کردن of خویش is expressed by the split خوی + ش (possessive suffix). Word order places the verb before the list of objects being negated.
segments.s389-g27: شوم here is a first‑person subjunctive/optative of شدن ('may I become' / 'I shall'), used as an emphatic or volitional opener; باز is the adverb 'again'; خواهم is the first‑person future/volitional auxiliary 'I will' or 'I intend to'. ز introduces the target/source (here marking Arjasp as the object of the following noun), ارجاسپ is the proper name, and کین is the noun 'vengeance'—together expressing the speaker's promise to take revenge on Arjasp.
segments.s389-g28: نمانم is the negative first‑person (subjunctive/volitional) of ماندن 'to remain' meaning 'I will not remain' or 'I shall not stay.' بر و بوم is a fixed collocation meaning 'land / homeland' and توران زمین specifies the place 'the land of Turan' (زمین further emphasizes 'land/country'). The preposition/locative sense is expressed by بر and the sequence denotes location from which he will not remain.
segments.s389-g29: به تخت آورم is 'I will bring (them) to the throne'—به marks the destination and آورم is the first‑person present/future of آوردن 'to bring'. خواهران را is the definite object (را marks it as definite/specific). ز بند means 'from bondage/chain' with ز + noun indicating origin/source of release.
segments.s389-g30: به بخت جهاندار شاه بلند is a prepositional phrase specifying the intended fate/destination: به + بخت 'to the fortune/destiny', جهاندار and شاه بلند are epithets in apposition ('world‑ruler, the lofty king') describing whose fortune it is. Word order places the destiny noun before its qualifying titles.
segments.s389-g31: This clause reports the speaker's reaction: آفرین کرد is the past tense 'he praised / he applauded', with گشتاسپ as the subject. برو at the start is an interjection/imperative 'go' or a short exclamation tied to the reported speech. و گفت is the connective 'and said', introducing what he uttered next; overall the clause sets up reported speech.
segments.s389-g32: که is the complementizer 'that' introducing the content of what was said. با تو 'with you' is the prepositional phrase; روان و خرد are the coordinated nouns 'soul and wisdom'. باد is the optative/subjunctive particle meaning 'may (it be)', and جفت is a predicative noun meaning 'paired / together' (i.e., 'may soul and wisdom be paired with you').

sentences.s389-s21: They bestowed a treasure on Esfandiyar: a certain crown, full of jewels and majestic.
sentences.s389-s22: A tumult arose from the king's court; from around the shining sun a darkness came.
sentences.s389-s23: Esfandiyar came from the portico onto the plain; he chose warriors from the gateway of the battlefield.
sentences.s389-s1: After that he came into the court-chamber and, in every way, put forward counsel to the king.
sentences.s389-s2: From Lohrasp and from the grudge of Farshidvard, (were) those famed on the day of battle.
sentences.s389-s3: Gushtasp said to him, 'O mighty one, you are joyful while your sisters are in bonds.'
sentences.s389-s4: Blessed is he who was slain at the place of vengeance; not he who was left bewildered in the grip of the Turks.
sentences.s389-s9: I entrust to you the imperial crown — that treasure gained without toil and the moon-like throne.
sentences.s389-s10: A place of worship is enough for me; my child is not with any other person.
sentences.s389-s11: Esfandiyar answered thus: 'That without you no one will appear in the world.'
sentences.s389-s12: In my father's presence I am but a single servant; my soul is filled by his command.
sentences.s389-s5: When they see us seated on the throne, what can someone who was once under (your) hand say?
sentences.s389-s6: I will weep over this shame while I live; I have cast fire into my innermost core.
sentences.s389-s7: I have accepted (this) from the Lofty Creator: if you go to Turan unharmed...
sentences.s389-s8: You will become a man at the dragon's moment and free your sisters from the Turks.
sentences.s389-s17: May God be your protector in your departure, and may your throne be restored upon your return.
sentences.s389-s18: At that time he will summon the army from every direction to the place that the evil mobad indicates.
sentences.s389-s19: From among them he selected twelve thousand horsemen — men deadly and full of hatred.
sentences.s389-s20: He bestowed treasure and dirhams upon them; to no one was he sullen or stingy in his giving.
sentences.s389-s13: I offer my body and soul to you; I do not desire my head, throne, or authority.
sentences.s389-s14: I will again take vengeance on Arjasp.
sentences.s389-s15: I will bring the sisters from bondage to the throne, to the fortune of the lofty world‑ruling king.
sentences.s389-s16: Gushtasp exclaimed (or applauded) and said, 'May soul and wisdom be with you together.'
